expectations.text: Is European Portuguese: uses 'queria' (and/or 'era') as the polite request form, NOT 'gostaria' (BP) or 'quereria' (textbook EP nobody uses)
expectations.evidence: Lead form is *Queria*. Also introduces *Era* alternative. Explicitly flags *quereria* as 'grammatically correct but never heard at a counter' — applies Native Usage Filter.

expectations.text: No BP forms slip in: no você (as the subject pronoun), no gerúndio for ongoing action (estar a + inf is EP)
expectations.evidence: Teacher notes explicit: 'EP only. Zero BP forms — gostaria, gostaria de, você, gerúndio all banned.' Dialogue uses no você or gerúndio.

expectations.text: All non-cognate Portuguese words are translated to English
expectations.evidence: Every dialogue line and vocabulary entry has parenthetical English translation

expectations.text: Uses 'Estudante' / 'Data' headers (worksheet conventions per project), or at least Portuguese cognate headers (not 'Student' / 'Date')
expectations.evidence: Both 'Estudante:' and 'Data:' header lines present at top with blanks

expectations.text: No fake accents on Portuguese words (real spellings only)
expectations.evidence: Teacher notes confirm: 'No accent inventions (P24): all spellings are real EP — queria, café, água, galão, pastel, obrigada.'

expectations.text: Generic learner audience — no specific roster student name (Marvin, Christian, Amanda, etc.)
expectations.evidence: Dialogue character is 'Ana' (generic). No reference to any roster student.
Dialogue is exactly 6 lines (3 from Empregado, 3 from Ana)

expectations.text: Lisbon-specific vocabulary present (bica, galão, pastel de nata, se faz favor) and explained
expectations.evidence: Vocabulary section includes bica, galão, meia de leite, pastel de nata, se faz favor — all defined inline
